content.problemStatement:
You’re given an array AAA containing NNN integers. Your task is to make Ai≥0A_i \geq 0Ai​≥0 for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.

To achieve that, you can perform the following operation:


Choose an index iii (1≤i<N1 \le i \lt N1≤i<N) and any integer XXX (XXX can be negative);
Subtract XXX from AiA_iAi​ and add XXX to Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​.

That is, set Ai:=Ai−XA_i:= A_i - XAi​:=Ai​−X and Ai+1:=Ai+1+XA_{i+1} := A_{i+1} + XAi+1​:=Ai+1​+X.
Find the **minimum** number of operations necessary to make Ai≥0A_i \geq 0Ai​≥0 for all iii.

If it's impossible to achieve this, output −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, denoting the length of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the minimum number of operations needed to make Ai≥0A_i \geq 0Ai​≥0 for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.

If it's not possible to achieve this no matter what, output −1-1−1 instead.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only possible operation is to add some XXX to A1A_1A1​ and subtract the same XXX from A2A_2A2​.
With just this, it's not possible to have both A1≥0A_1\geq 0A1​≥0 and A2≥0A_2\geq 0A2​≥0 simultaneously.
Test case 222: One way of using two operations is as follows:

Initially, A=[−3,2,1]A = [-3, 2, 1]A=[−3,2,1].
Choose i=2i=2i=2 and X=−1X=-1X=−1. After this operation, A=[−3,3,0]A=[-3,3,0]A=[−3,3,0].
Choose i=1i=1i=1 and X=−3X=-3X=−3. After this operation, A=[0,0,0]A=[0,0,0]A=[0,0,0] and we're done.

It can be shown that using only one operation is not enough.
